The first Penetrators started coming off the production line in 3053. Intended to act as a fire support 'Mech, The PTR-4F Penetrator sacrifices two of its Medium Pulse Lasers and the ER Large Lasers, freeing enough tonnage to add two LRM-10 missile launchers. The LRM-10s were then mated to Artemis IV fire control systems for added accuracy.
yangsThoughts: An interesting rebuild, it swaps out the energy hardpoints in the arms for missiles, which allows for some interesting builds, but you'll have to deal with ammo supplies.

details: Designed by engineers at the New Avalon Institute of Science in an attempt to copy the Mad Cat, while not as powerful as the OmniMech it emulates, the Rakshasa is a formidable 'Mech with an undeserved bad reputation.  Since the Inner Sphere first saw the Mad Cat they could only marvel at such a potent mix of speed, armor and firepower and how they themselves could produce a copy of such a design. Unlike the Draconis Combine whose reverse engineering efforts ultimately focused on the Clans OmniPod technology, technicians and students at the New Avalon Institute of Science labored for five long years attempting to specifically replicate the Mad Cat and its iconic primary configuration, rumors insisting the final breakthrough being the in-depth analysis of a captured Mad Cat. Its designers settled on calling the result the Rakshasa after a mythical creature that mimicked its adversaries abilities and appearance. The Rakshasa is powered by a GM 375 XL engine, giving it comparable ground speed to the 'Mech it attempts to emulate, and built on a GM Marauder Special MD Endo Steel chassis to save weight and allow the 'Mech to carry as similar a weapons payload as possible to the Mad Cat. The Rakshasa also carries eleven and a half tons of StarGuard ferro-fibrous armor giving it slightly less than maximum armor protection. A total of fifteen double heat sinks are woefully inadequate for the Rakshasa's heavy energy weapon loadout, requiring careful heat management on part of its pilot. Along with its visual similarity, offensively the Rakshasa tries to clone the weapons array of the Mad Cat Prime with boxy LRM launcher torsos supported by double barrelled laser-carrying arms.
yangsThoughts: I get what they were trying for with immitating the Timber Wolf, but the lack of Clan-Spec weaponry really hinders this mech, the obvious solution being to actually give it the weapons loadout of the mech it's trying to be if we have any spare.

yangsThoughts: Now this is a better setup, instead of trying to just blindly copy something you can't properly emulate, actually try to do something different.

The primary weapon is a rotary autocannon/5. This is backed up at shorter ranges by a Large Pulse Laser and for point-blank combat, the 'Mech carries four Medium Pulse Lasers.
yangsThoughts: Another redesign, this time a production version, that stops trying to be a Timber Wolf and starts trying to be something different and effective.

details: The Salamander is an assault fire support 'Mech that is built to carry out a mission similar to the Awesome: long range fire support and bombardment. The Salamander is powered by a Pitban 320 XL engine and is built on a Chariot Type IV Endo Steel chassis, allowing the 'Mech to dedicate over half of its weight to its weapons and the equipment needed to carry out its mission. The Salamander is armored with fifteen and a half tons of armor, which is heavy even for an assault class fire support 'Mech.  The Salamander's arsenal centers around its primary long-range weapons systems; three Doombud LRM-20 Racks. This heavy LRM payload allows the Salamander to put out one and a half times the downrange fire of an Archer. Additionally, the Salamander can sustain a barrage for three minutes at its maximum rate of fire, which is an impressive feat for a dedicated fire support 'Mech. For close range defense, the 'Mech carries two Defiance B3M medium lasers.
yangsThoughts: A nice powerful long range fire support mech, basically an Archer on steroids. It's kind of slow, but no worse than the Archer, and that massive barrage is really useful. If you have the funds for it, I'd love to see what this baby can do with a full set of Clan-spec missile racks.

details: The Salamander is an assault fire support 'Mech that is built to carry out a mission similar to the Awesome: long range fire support and bombardment. The Salamander is powered by a Pitban 320 XL engine and is built on a Chariot Type IV Endo Steel chassis, allowing the 'Mech to dedicate over half of its weight to its weapons and the equipment needed to carry out its mission. The Salamander is armored with fifteen and a half tons of armor, which is heavy even for an assault class fire support 'Mech. The 6T model also makes the Salamander more powerful at close range. One of the LRM-20 launchers have been removed to make room for a pair of medium pulse lasers and a large pulse laser. This makes the Salamander very potent if forced into a short-range firefight.
yangsThoughts: It's still got most of the hardpoints for missiles, even if it's only using two of them. This thing has enough short range firepower to be a serious threat to anything that gets too close.

details: The Thunder is a product of the aftermath of the Fourth Succession War. The 'Mech was built to make the most of new technologies that had made their way to the Capellan Confederation at the beginning of the 3050s. This was done by building the 'Mech around a Magna 350 XL engine to maximize the available weight in the 'Mech. The Thunder also uses double heat sinks to make it capable of handling a high heat load from its weapons systems. The 'Mech is well protected with thirteen and a half tons of armor. Its weapons payload, and the design in general, uses a mix of advanced and off the shelf parts. This balanced mix makes the Thunder a valuable asset on the modern battlefield. The primary weapon on the Thunder is the massive Kali Yama Big Bore Autocannon/20 which is capable of devastating most lighter 'Mechs with a single shot. This is backed up with three highly accurate Ceres Arms Model W Medium Pulse Lasers. Finally, for token long range defense capabilities, and giving the 'Mech limited indirect fire capabilities, the Thunder carries a Sian/Ceres Jaguar LRM-5 launcher.
yangsThoughts: It can mount a decent weapons array and is nice and fast, only real problem is that it could use a weaponry refresh to bring it up to more recent standards.

details: The Thunder is a product of the aftermath of the Fourth Succession War. The Thunder also uses double heat sinks to make it capable of handling a high heat load from its weapons systems. The 'Mech is well protected with thirteen and a half tons of armor. Its weapons payload, and the design in general, uses a mix of advanced and off the shelf parts. This balanced mix makes the Thunder a valuable asset on the modern battlefield. The 2L model of the Thunder upgrades the design with some of the latest technology, making it a highly destructive close combat 'Mech. The Autocannon/20 has been replaced with an LB 20-X AC which acts like a giant 'Mech-scale shotgun. The 'Mech is also armed with two ER Medium Lasers and an ER Small Laser. Additionally, the LRM-5 has been dropped in favor of a Streak SRM-6 launcher for additional close range firepower. The final touch on the 'Mech is addition of a Guardian ECM suite which makes the 'Mech much harder for an enemy unit to target.
yangsThoughts: This version has gotten that weaponry refresh and is overall better at close range, though the lack of any significant long range firepower means you'll have to be cautious to avoid getting sniped.

This was done by building the 'Mech around a Magna 350 XL engine to maximize the available weight in the 'Mech. The Thunder also uses double heat sinks to make it capable of handling a high heat load from its weapons systems. The 'Mech is well protected with thirteen and a half tons of armor. Its weapons payload, and the design in general, uses a mix of advanced and off the shelf parts. This balanced mix makes the Thunder a valuable asset on the modern battlefield. This version of the Thunder replaces the heavy autocannon with a Sian/Ceres Hellcat Thunderbolt 20 missile launcher. It also mounts three Ceres Arms ER Medium Lasers and a single Ceres Arms Model X Medium X-Pulse Laser for close combat. The XL engine is smaller than the standard Thunders engine, reducing the top speed to 64 km/h. To offset this loss of speed, the C4 mounts six Anderson High Propulsion 12s Improved Jump Jets. Built on an Endo Steel chassis, the rest of the 'Mech is covered in thirteen tons of armor.
yangsThoughts: An interesting variant, that thunderbolt lets it hit hard at distance, and the much bigger weight allowance allows for a lot more firepower options. If you plan on using the thunderbolt for indirect fire a lot, you might want to pull some of the jump jets off for more missiles.

details: The Diomede is an early post-Jihad IndustrialMech that was produced by Curtiss Hydroponics of Silver. Starting with the D-M3D-3 model, this massive ConstructionMech was designed to handle the rigors of harsh realities that face potential customers. In addition to handling potential construction tasks, the Diomede can also serve as a SecurityMech if needed. Because of this combat equipment, the Republic of the Sphere requires users to pay large amounts in licensing fees to help keep it from falling into the wrong hands. Built to circumvent the Republic's restrictions on BattleMech production, this Diomede is a cheap assault 'Mech introduced in 3092.[6] None of the standard industrial equipment remains in this variant. It has been replaced by a pair of medium lasers, twin machine guns, and a Imperator Gauss rifle. The 'Mech retains the twin MML-7 launchers. The tracks used by the standard Diomede remain.
yangsThoughts: Is that construction equipment in my mechbay? I'll grant you that it's got an ok weapons loadout for an assault mech, just barely, but it's so crazily slow that you're gonna be dead before you reach the enemy unless you put more long range weapons on there.

details: Originally designed for the Republic of the Sphere, the Doloire was created as the Republic Armed Forces flagship OmniMech. The Republic utilized scientists from Clan enclaves within The Republic to design the assault OmniMech as a powerful and important symbol of cooperation between all citizens, Inner Sphere and Clan. The Doloire was first produced within The Republic in 3121, being produced by StarCorps on Terra. The design would continue into service and secretly be part of the rebuilding of the RAF behind the walls of Fortress Republic. Elements outside of Fortress, including mercenaries, remaining Republican forces, and invaders of former Republic space claimed some examples of the design. The Doloire was designed using advanced forms of Clan and Inner Sphere technology of the era. Designed as an OmniMech with thirty-two tons of pod space for weapons and equipment, engineers used Endo-Composite for its chassis as well as a durable Clan 320-rated Extra Light Fusion Engine. While not the fastest among its peers on the battlefield, the 'Mech is able to handle a respectable amount of incoming fire with its nearly-fifteen tons of ArcShield VII Mk.7 standard armor. The 'Mech's fixed armaments are a pair of Clan Series 2f Extended Range Medium Lasers mounted in the center torso. The primary configuration of the Doloire mounts a pair of highly-accurate Clan Large Pulse Lasers mounted each of the 'Mech's arms. Their accuracy is increased with the presence of Actuator Enhancement Systems placed in each arm. For long-ranged attacks, the prime configuration includes a Clan-tech Gauss rifle with sixteen rounds of ammunition found in the right torso. To help with the 'Mech's heat, four additional Clan-made Double Heat Sinks are added to the configuration. Rounding off the primary's equipment is an ECM Suite
yangsThoughts: A nice flexible omnimech with advanced construction, good armor, potent clan-spec weaponry and versatile electronics.

In the right is a rotary autocannon/5, drawing from four tons of CASE II-protected ammunition located in the right torso. Five additional Clan-made heat sinks round out the equipment for the B.
yangsThoughts: A long range specialized layout, watch out for the heat output on this one.

yangsThoughts: A heavy missile boat setup, this thing is good at either long or short range, but you'll have to watch out for the ammo explosions and your supplies.

details: The prototype of the BattleAxe originally appeared in 2459, and relied on the standard armament of the time: lasers and missiles. Over the next fifteen years, the speed was increased by a third and the mass of the cockpit and armor were reduced. In the late 2460s, unsure of the BattleAxe's maneuverability, the introduction of the WSP-1 Wasp inspired designers to mount experimental jump jets onto the upper legs, allowing it to vault over anything it could not run through. Unfortunately, MechWarriors had to go through training for the new jump jets and the landing process, as many died early on. The Federated Suns subsequently switched the Large Lasers in the arms for new Particle Projection Cannons. The BattleAxe only has nine tons of armor, which is comparatively light for a 'Mech of its size. Massive overheating problems, and the introduction of the Hammerhands, eventually ended the operational service of the BattleAxe. The last BattleAxes, considered ancient, were destroyed in a desperate defense of Robinson against conventionally equipped Combat Vehicle formations during the Second Succession War. The original production variant of this 'Mech, the BKX-7K, utilizes two massive Donal Particle Projection Cannons, which were relatively new at the time. Supporting these massive cannons are two Federated 5-Shot LRM-5 launchers with one ton of ammo split between them, as well as a Harpoon-6 SRM-6. These secondary weapons give the BattleAxe something to hit with at all ranges.
yangsThoughts: A true classic antique, from the same era as the Mackie, I feel priviledged just being able to see this thing in my mechbay. The weapons layout for this thing is still viable, similar to the WarHammer but with a little bit less firepower.

details: The Bellerophon was a failed prototype BattleMech developed in 2442 by what would later be known as Defiance Industries. Designed during the infancy of BattleMech technology, Defiance Industries used what later was known as Primitive Technology to create the Bellerophon. It was envisioned that the design would complement the Mackie with its higher speed, while having nearly as much armor as its companion. Using technology based on the original Mackie prototype, the design inherited a host of design flaws from its predecessor. Chief among these were stability issues: the weapon arrangement would amplify its balance problems despite numerous improvements to the gyro system. After four prototypes were produced, the program was canceled due to the Terran Hegemony's lack of interest in the design. The design incorporated a pair of Large Lasers coupled with an SRM-4 which it would use to provide supporting fire for the Mackie. However, the two large lasers were mounted in the right arm, and the small SRM launcher found in the left arm was hardly able to keep the design balanced and upright, particularly while maneuvering. The Bellerophon's 14 heat sinks were not enough to offset any heat spikes should the MechWarrior choose to fire all their weapons at once, or even fire the two Large Lasers in tandem. This example is one of the rebuilt versions built during the Jihad, and uses Standard armor, chassis and engine instead of the original Primitive tech.
yangsThoughts: I know I asked you to bring me any special mechs and antiques you found, but why THIS thing? I suppose the rebuild using modern gyros at least fixed the balance so it's only under armed as opposed to also unable to stand up.

details: The second exclusive Federated Suns BattleMech, the Hammerhands was developed in 2475 after a decade reviewing the performance and complaints received about the earlier BattleAxe. Little more than a heavier and slower version of the BattleAxe, the design received its name based on the impressive weapons carried in each arm, serving to trigger the Federated Suns' seeming love affair with autocannons. Much like its sibling design, the Hammerhands went on to influence and be outdone by the immensely popular Warhammer, causing it to fade into history, all but forgotten. The Achernar designers' solution for the high heat generated by the BattleAxe's PPCs was to replace them with a pair of Mydron Model B Autocannon/10s backed up by ChisComp 38 medium lasers in each arm, while retaining the earlier design's shoulder-mounted Harpoon-6 SRM-6. Carrying four tons of ammunition spread between the arms and torsos, the Hammerhands autocannons were rarely silenced during most Age of War era conflicts.
yangsThoughts: Now this is a true classic mech, they don't design them like this anymore, although that's probably a good thing for pilot survivability. It's got two powerful autocannons, and some basic additional weapons, but I'd say it could really use updating to the latest ultra autocannons or rotary autocannons to get some proper damage. If you've got them, you could even put some clan gausses on there.

details: The second BattleMech model introduced by the Free Worlds League, the Hector entered service in 2471,[2] only one year after the original Icarus. Built by Corean Enterprises MacAdams-Suharno on Stewart, it used a simple design suited for mass production and quickly became the workhorse of the Free Worlds League Military's 'Mech regiments. Limited by the Primitive Technology it was built with, the Hector's performance was generally considered lackluster. Its good top speed came at a big price; the large engine took up so much mass that the 'Mech ended up undergunned. It also had endurance problems as its twelve tons of primitive armor did not provide adequate protection to deal with heavy opponents and it also lacked enough heat sinks for its armament. Overall the Hector proved largely unpopular in its time but was just effective enough to last into the 2520s when it was taken out of service in favor of newer, better designs. No Hectors survived the Age of War, making it one of the earliest lost 'Mech designs. The Hector carries an I.W.W. Large Laser in each arm, giving it a respectable punch at the price of easily overheating. A curious design choice was the inclusion of Sperry Browning machine guns mounted on its upper legs. Meant to deter infantry attacks, the placement of the guns, and especially their ammo bins, in the legs had the opposite effect - enemy infantry soon began specifically targeting the Hector's legs to cripple them via ammunition explosions. This specific mech is a modern reconstruction of the original design, mounting standard equipment and having additional weaponry.
yangsThoughts: Somebody must really like this classic design as they rebuilt it using standard equipment, and used the weight savings to put more weaponry on. It's at least more viable than the original setup, but you'll pretty much have to upgrade this thing heavily to make it useful.

details: The Kyudo was developed by Martinson Armaments early in the history of BattleMechs, having been unveiled in 2443. Lighter, faster, and more importantly, much cheaper than the Mackie, the Terran Hegemony purchased the design in its intended role of supporting the more powerful Mackie and assault tank units. Fierce competition saw the mech relegated to second echelon forces before the Reunification War. Martinson was destroyed during the Amaris Civil War and the Kyudo eventually faded into obscurity. The 45-ton Kyudo tops out at 86 km/h, thanks to its VOX 225 fusion engine. It's Chariot Type E endo steel chassis and internal machinery is protected by eight tons of Armorscale ferro-fibrous armor, and has CASE to protect the MechWarrior in the event of an ammunition explosion. 10 double heat sinks make heat a secondary concern. As a support 'Mech, the Kyudo mounts a Conan/20 LRM-20 that is guided by an Artemis IV FCS, which enables it to provide accurate direct and indirect fire support. To back it up, the Kyudo has a single Sorenstein ER large laser that is as effective in close as it is downrange.
yangsThoughts: A perfectly solid if old mech design, it mounts a nice big lrm rack and a decently powerful laser so it can easily cover for a larger mech.

The rediscovery of the Hegemony Memory Core led to the resurfacing of the Kyudo. Irian BattleMechs Unlimited upgraded a maintenance facility on the planet Lopez to be able to produce an upgraded variant of that 'Mech in 3079. The missile launcher is replaced with a pair of Artemis-guided MML-7s, though they only have two tons of ammunition.
yangsThoughts: A versatile upgrade on a classic design, this is still just as fast and effective as before, but it's more flexible with the MMLs.

details: The Rampage was a BattleMech produced by the Rim Worlds Republic. The 'Mech was originally built and used as a standard line unit, but it was upgraded to a fast assault 'Mech in 2767, following Stefan Amaris' successful coup that installed him as the nominal leader of the Terran Hegemony. With the occupation of Star League R&D facilities, his Periphery nation was finally able to produce the SLDF's advanced weaponry. The Rampage's upgrade utilized Endo Steel and an XL engine to save weight. As engineers debated the best way to make the Rampage faster, they settled on installing the new MASC system, which accelerates the 85-ton 'Mech to 86 km/h in bursts. While its speed gives it a measure of protection, the design also sports a massive sixteen tons of PanzerSlab 9 armor. The primary weapon of the Rampage is the Lubalin LB 10-X AC. Two tons of ammunition allow it to switch between standard and cluster shots. It also has an array of lasers, namely the Tronel XIII Large Pulse Laser housed in its chest, and two Maxell DT medium lasers, one on each side of the pulse laser. In addition to the autocannon for long-range fire, the Rampage has an Artemis IV Fire Control System-guided Jackson Dart LRM-10 launcher with a single ton of ammo. To strengthen its short range firepower, three Marklin Mini Streak SRM-2 launchers are bundled together in the left torso with a ton of ammo to share between them. For defense against missiles, the Rampage mounted a Buzzsaw anti-missile system. Against infantry, a Zippo Mark X Anti-Personnel Flamer is installed; a popular piece of equipment for the Rim Worlds' civilian pacification efforts. Thirteen double heat sinks keeps heat levels in check.
yangsThoughts: This thing has a bad reputation for the attrocities it unleashed with its flamers during the civil war, but the actual design is solid.

Introduced in 2750 and built in limited numbers prior to introduction of 5G, the 4G Rampage was similar to the -5G upgrade. It lacked the missile launchers and one medium laser, but added a Gauss rifle for concentrated long-range fire. This required the minimum amount of double heat sinks and slightly less armor.
yangsThoughts: Better at long range sniping but a little less armored, I think this design is overall better than the standard design.

details: The Shogun began production before the Star League fell and eventually was thought to be lost to the Succession Wars. The 'Mech suddenly reappeared when Wolf's Dragoons showed up in 3005, bringing a sizable number of Shoguns with them. The 'Mech was intended to be used as an urban combat 'Mech where its jumping distance and slow speed makes it able to easily traverse the close confines of a city. Though the Dragoons brought Shoguns with them from the Clan Homeworlds, they could not reach a deal with any manufacturer that met their desires, so by 3068 the total count of the 'Mech in the Inner Sphere was reduced to two, both of which were in the hands of Wannamaker's Widowmakers, taken as salvage from the Dragoons. Though built for close combat, the weapons payload on the Shogun is built for long range combat. The 'Mech carries as its primary weapon a Magna Firestar ER PPC and two Coventry Star Fire LRM-15 launchers which allow the 'Mech to engage an enemy very effectively at long ranges. The 'Mech does carry two Thunderstroke SRM-6 launchers for close combat, allowing the 'Mech to find any weak spots in an enemy's armor when they do get to close ranges with the Shogun.
yangsThoughts: A good firesupport and long range sniper mech, the paired LRMs and ER PPC allow it to engage well at long range.

The original 2777 Shogun model is similar to the 3050's era SHG-2F, adding Artemis IV fire control systems to the LRM racks. The SRM-6 racks are replaced with four Defiance Streak SRM-2 launchers, which share a single ton of ammunition. A head-mounted Blankenburg Medium Pulse Laser and a Magna Mk II medium laser in the center torso rounds out the weapons. Armorscale ferro-fibrous armor and CASE protect the 'Mech.
yangsThoughts: A lot more hardpoints for mounting things will let you reconfigure this thing much better, Those paired SRM2s aren't really needed now that we can make larger streak racks.

A complete redesign of the Shogun using Clan technology, the Shogun C is armed with two LRM-20 missile racks with an Artemis IV fire control system for greater missile accuracy. The 'Mech also carries an ER PPC and four Streak SRM-6 launchers, giving it a devastating close range damage capability.
yangsThoughts: Now this is more like it, those light clan missiles really increase the damage output of this mech without increasing weight.

details: The Striker was originally commissioned by the Star League Defense Force high command in 2571 for non-Royal SLDF units (those units originally donated by Great Houses to the SLDF). Along with the Stalker,[5] the Striker became one of the most common 'Mechs in service. The Striker was built with the intent of being both rugged and reliable, but more importantly, its production allowed House Lords to reserve their precious and limited numbers of assault BattleMechs for their own personal armies. In this way, the Striker may have been the show of good faith the House Lords needed to make the Star League work. In spite of the 'Mech's dependability, the Striker was marginal compared with later assault BattleMechs. It was eventually relegated to serving in militia units, with the highest concentration ending up on the rimward edge of the Terran Hegemony. After the destruction of the Stormvanger lines in 2867, fewer than 300 Strikers were known to exist as of 3058. The Striker has an arsenal that is capable of engaging an enemy at a variety of ranges. At maximum range, the Striker brings its HellStar PPC and a Class-5 Pontiac Autocannon to bear. As the enemy closes, it can also make use of its Defiance B3L Large Laser, though the unit's pilot will need to monitor their heat generation carefully. Finally, for close range defense, the Striker has three Hellion-b II medium lasers.
yangsThoughts: A dependable classic Assault 'Mech, decent speed, but not very good cooling for how much weaponry it mounts. Armor's also a bit thin but we can shift things around fairly easily.

Along with the Stalker,[5] the Striker became one of the most common 'Mechs in service. The 2D variant of the Striker is a very basic upgrade of the design used by the Capellan Confederation Armed Forces. The PPC has been upgraded to an ER PPC, the three medium lasers have been replaced with a pair of Medium Pulse Lasers, and the 'Mech has had three heat sinks removed. The remaining heat sinks have been upgraded to double heat sinks. The remaining available space has been used to add two tons of armor to the 'Mech.
yangsThoughts: A nice simple upgrade to a venerable design, the ER PPC gives it some good ranged combat ability, and the change to medium pulse lasers increases accuracy. This is the best you're gonna get without putting some seriously updated tech on here.

details: A design that started out flawed due to political maneuvering before the Davion Civil War in 1515. The unit suffered from poor workmanship for its autocannon feed and was burderned by it's initial use of primitive armor and an engine too light to properly propel it. This version is the result of tinkering and updating during the civil war updating the tech to standard grade and using the weight savings of that change to increase the weaponry mounted. Final weaponry count include an LRM-5, a Class 5 autocannon, an SRM-4, two medium lasers and a small laser mounted in the head.
yangsThoughts: This improved version at least has decent tech, but you'll want to consider upgrading the weapons even more or adding some more heat sinks, this thing doesn't have the best cooling system from how old it is.

details: The Chameleon was designed to help a new MechWarrior master the skills of maneuvering and heat management, with a top speed of 97.2 km/h and a jumping distance of 180 meters. The 'Mech also had an almost all-energy-weapon loadout, but was cooled by only 10 single heat sinks; this paucity of freezers forces MechWarriors to concentrate on the 'Mech's heat levels. In some instances, the Chameleon had been forced into combat situations, notably during the Dragoon Civil War and the World of Blake's conquest of Terra. The Chameleon carried a basic arsenal that allowed a MechWarrior-in-training to learn the skill of heat management. The 'Mech's reliable low-tech arsenal proved effective when forced into combat; the primary weapon was a Cyclops Eye large laser, backed up by two Intek medium lasers, and a trio of Defiance B3S small lasers. To make the threat of overheating more real to the new MechWarrior, the 'Mech carried two Scatter Gun Light machine guns that could be used for anti-infantry training.
yangsThoughts: A decent loadout for a training mech, but it's got an awful heat profile for actual combat.

details: The Chameleon was designed to help a new MechWarrior master the skills of maneuvering and heat management, with a top speed of 97.2 km/h and a jumping distance of 180 meters. The 'Mech also had an almost all-energy-weapon loadout, but was cooled by only 10 single heat sinks; this paucity of freezers forces MechWarriors to concentrate on the 'Mech's heat levels. In some instances, the Chameleon had been forced into combat situations, notably during the Dragoon Civil War and the World of Blake's conquest of Terra. The 7W variant of the Chameleon looked to use the 'Mech in a full-time combat role. The 'Mech upgraded the large laser to an ER large laser. Additionally, the machine guns and small lasers were removed and replaced with a medium pulse laser and double heat sinks. J.B. BattleMechs allowed pilots to turn off the heat management software for battlefield flexibility.
yangsThoughts: An improvement on the energy handling, and improved range make this way better.

The weapons complement was changed considerably. An ER large laser and standard medium laser were mounted in the right arm. Another medium laser remained in the left arm. Two medium X-pulse lasers were mounted in the right torso, while another was mounted in the left. A pair of machine guns were still carried in the center torso but now shared a CASE II–protected half-ton magazine in the left torso. To make room for all these improvements, a 300 XL engine was used. The heat sinks were upgrade to twelve doubles. Nine tons of standard armor protected this variant.
yangsThoughts: That xl engine really makes room for a lot more weaponry while putting in some more heat sinks to manage the load.

details: A mech based on the Raven, this mech is designed to integrate stealth equipment to avoid detection and TAG lasers to improve damage done to targets. This variant mounts two ER Large Lasers and two ER Medium Lasers for good ranged damage.
yangsThoughts: Fast and Light, that stealth equipment is pretty useful, though its a bit heavy.

details: A mech based on the Raven, this mech is designed to integrate stealth equipment to avoid detection and TAG lasers to improve damage done to targets. This variant mounts two snub-nose PPCs and two Medium Lasers for improved close in damage.
yangsThoughts: Fast and Light, that stealth equipment is pretty useful, though its a bit heavy.

details: The Exterminator is a BattleMech that was designed in 2630 for the most dubious, but necessary, of purposes: Head Hunting. The Exterminator was outfitted with a special Null-Signature System, heat baffles and, in some cases, a Chameleon Light Polarization Shield. These advanced systems allowed it to easily infiltrate enemy lines. Once the Exterminator breached the enemy's rear, its mission was to find and destroy as much of the enemy's command structure as possible. This mission profile included attacking mobile headquarters, field bases and command level officers. Rather than be part of the standard formation, Exterminators were attached to a regimental or higher headquarters unit, often as a fifth member of the command lance itself, before being assigned to individual units. A regiment was considered lucky to have more than one Exterminator, and it was not unusual for its parent unit to pull all assigned Exterminators and pool them for a specific mission. The weapons used by the Exterminator are not nearly as unique or high-tech as the onboard electronics. The primary weapons of the Exterminator are four Averell Highpoint medium lasers, two in each arm. For long-range engagements, the 'Mech also carries a Deadeye-10 LRM-10 launcher with 12 reloads in the center torso. These weapons are backed up by a single Dinatech Mark III small laser mounted in the head for when engagements close to point-blank range and, for defense against missile attacks, the Exterminator has a Buzzsaw anti-missile system in the right torso, with enough ammo to intercept 12 incoming volleys.
yangsThoughts: A stealthy assassin, I had thought all of this model had been destroyed in the succession wars, but I guess we've gotten lucky and found a rare survivor.

details: Entering service in 2669, the Firefly was one of the standard light 'Mechs of the Star League Defense Force in its later years, with a small volume also entering service with all the Member State militaries. Although too slow when compared to other 'Mechs of similar weight to act as a scout, the Firefly instead served as a light support 'Mech, its superior armor and armaments allowing it to act as the anvil used by the rest of its lance to crush enemy scout forces. The 'Mech was also deployed independently in urban environments where its jump jets gave it a decisive edge against infantry and light combat vehicles and is useful in a guerrilla fighter role. First built in 2679, the standard Star League Defense Force model utilizes an XL engine, double heat sinks, and ferro-fibrous armor to reduce weight. A battery of four small lasers, three medium lasers, and an LRM-5 provide significant offensive capabilities for a recon 'Mech. TAG enhances its ability to spot for artillery, while CASE protects from ammunition explosion.
yangsThoughts: I thought all of these had been either degraded by the long years before the clan invasion or destroyed in the inner sphere, but apparently someone is still making a small number of there, although I'm not sure who would have the schematics for it.

details: Entering service in 2669, the Firefly was one of the standard light 'Mechs of the Star League Defense Force in its later years, with a small volume also entering service with all the Member State militaries. Although too slow when compared to other 'Mechs of similar weight to act as a scout, the Firefly instead served as a light support 'Mech, its superior armor and armaments allowing it to act as the anvil used by the rest of its lance to crush enemy scout forces. The 'Mech was also deployed independently in urban environments where its jump jets gave it a decisive edge against infantry and light combat vehicles and is useful in a guerrilla fighter role. Built by the Star League in Exile at Strana Mechty MechWorks, the 3SLE is almost identical to the original 3A, carrying three Martell medium lasers, four Martell small lasers, and a Coventry Five-Tube LRM-5 for offensive firepower. The only difference is the replacement of the SLDF standard TAG and reduction of armor to mount a Guardian ECM suite.
yangsThoughts: I've never seen this layout before, I'm not sure who built it, either, but the setup seems like a slight variant of the standard star league era model.

details: The Lancelot was originally conceived in response to a Star League Defense Force contract for a medium BattleMech that could operate with minimal logistical support, was fast enough to take part in mobile operations, and packed sufficient firepower to be a viable threat on the battlefield. With this criteria in mind, Krupp Technologies attempted to combine the speed of a medium 'Mech with the survivability of a heavy 'Mech in the Lancelot. Despite the use of a lightweight engine, the production Lancelot ended up in the heavyweight class, but the SLDF was impressed enough with the unit's speed and firepower during its trial runs to make an initial order of 250 in 2581. The weapons carried by the Lancelot cover almost the entire range of energy weapons available and gave it firepower equivalent to that found in many assault 'Mechs. Its primary armament is a Kinslaughter PPC mounted in the right side of its torso. The Kinslaughter has a tendency to generate more heat than other particle cannons if not properly maintained. This is backed up by two Krupp Model 32 Large Lasers in rotating arm mounts, known among MechWarriors as 'Fur Burners' for their excellent performance, and a single Krupp Model 2 medium laser in the chest.
yangsThoughts: Nice find, Commander, a repairable original Star League era model Lancelot. That rangefinder that matches the one on the rifleman will be helpful in shooting things from the safety of distance.

details: The Lancelot was originally conceived in response to a Star League Defense Force contract for a medium BattleMech that could operate with minimal logistical support, was fast enough to take part in mobile operations, and packed sufficient firepower to be a viable threat on the battlefield. With this criteria in mind, Krupp Technologies attempted to combine the speed of a medium 'Mech with the survivability of a heavy 'Mech in the Lancelot. Despite the use of a lightweight engine, the production Lancelot ended up in the heavyweight class, but the SLDF was impressed enough with the unit's speed and firepower during its trial runs to make an initial order of 250 in 2581. This prototype Lancelot variant was produced by Krupp prior to Amaris' seizure of Terra. An upgrade of the LNC25-01, it uses an Endo-Steel chassis and ferro-fibrous armor. The weapons array is same, except the large lasers are Extended Range Type and four jump jets are added for mobility. Early field tests revealed that the inclusion of jump jets did little to improve the design, with most pilots requesting either more armoring or more heat sinks instead.
yangsThoughts: An interesting prototype version of this mech, I think the original pilots are right, and either more armor, more heatsinks or more guns would be better.

Introduced by Kindraa Smythe-Jewel, the all-Clan tech Lancelot C carried three Kolibri Delta Series Large Pulse Lasers into battle as a light 'Mech hunter. Sixteen double heat sinks and nine and a half tons of armor protected the Lancelot C.
yangsThoughts: Now this is the sort of mech that really benefits from the Clan's superior energy weapons. You might want to consider swapping out a large pulse for some smaller weapons to improve heat handling.

Seventeen heat sinks offset the heat generated by these weapons. The Lancelot C 2 can also sprint at 120 km/h thanks to a MASC system.
yangsThoughts: An interesting change, though I'd probably replace the flamers with small pulse lasers or something.

details: The Night Hawk was designed as a fast reaction BattleMech for hunting down Periphery raiders that had managed to penetrate the front lines. Mountain Wolf BattleMechs submitted their proposal for the Night Hawk, their first BattleMech, to the Star League Defense Force in 2655, and after many months of trials, the 'Mech was put into full production in 2656. Production continued for almost 300 years until 2945, when the factory producing the Night Hawk was destroyed and did not see production again until 3055. The Night Hawk is an impressive light 'Mech on the modern battlefield, with a top speed of 97.2 km/h that is provided by a GM 210 XL extralight engine. For protection from enemy fire, the Night Hawk has seven tons of ProtecTech Light armor which can defend it from fire from most other light 'Mechs. Finally, to dissipate the vast amount of heat from its all energy weapons arsenal, the Night Hawk uses 12 double heat sinks. The Night Hawk carries an all energy weapons arsenal to allow it to operate in independent, long-term operations. The primary long range weapon on the Night Hawk is a Maxwell ER large laser that is backed up by a Defiance B3L large laser. Finally, for close range combat, the Night Hawk has a Defiance P5M medium pulse laser.
yangsThoughts: a nicely powerful light mech, you may find it to be a bit under armed for heavy combat though.

details: The largest BattleMech ever built by HildCo Interplanetary, the Pillager is built as a larger version of the Victor, and is intended for assaulting urban centers and hardened fortifications. These design parameters were paramount as the SLDF mobilized to wage the Reunification War. Later Pillagers were upgraded with more cutting-edge Star League technology. Production of the Pillager halted not because its facility was destroyed, but the advanced Gauss rifle and XL engine production lines had been obliterated by the end of the Second Succession War. Curiously, HildCo did not restart production of the original PLG-1N, rather the Pillager lines only began production again in 3058 when advanced components were once again available for the PLG-3Z. The original Pillager, designed in 2575 and fielded for the first two decades of the Reunification War, uses a standard fusion engine instead of the extralight model that went into production. Other limitations include a 60 meter jump range and 17 tons of armor. The weapons suite was also different, relying on twin torso mounted Autocannon/20s, a single SRM-4, and three medium lasers; one mounted in each arm and a head-mounted laser. Sixteen single heat sinks kept the heat under control.
yangsThoughts: A decent close in brawling design, the lack of light engine seriously cuts down on the amount of weaponry it can field.

details: The largest BattleMech ever built by HildCo Interplanetary, the Pillager is built as a larger version of the Victor, and is intended for assaulting urban centers and hardened fortifications. These design parameters were paramount as the SLDF mobilized to wage the Reunification War. Later Pillagers were upgraded with more cutting-edge Star League technology. Production of the Pillager halted not because its facility was destroyed, but the advanced Gauss rifle and XL engine production lines had been obliterated by the end of the Second Succession War. Curiously, HildCo did not restart production of the original PLG-1N, rather the Pillager lines only began production again in 3058 when advanced components were once again available for the PLG-3Z. The PLG-3Z Pillager is built using a lightweight Vlar 300 XL engine, which weighs half as much as a standard fusion engine and gives the Pillager a top speed of 54 km/h. The small mass of the engine allows the 'Mech to mount an obscene array of weapons. The 'Mech's mobility is enhanced with three HildCo Model 13 jump jets that allow it to jump up to ninety meters. The Pillager has excellent armor protection at nineteen and a half tons and has fourteen double heat sinks that provide more than enough heat dissipation capability for the Pillager's needs. The primary weapons on the Pillager were a pair of Poland Main Model A Gauss rifles, which allowed the Pillager to engage an enemy at up to six hundred and sixty meters. The Gauss rifles were supported at intermediate ranges by a Defiance B3L large laser. For close range combat, the Pillager had four Ceres Arms medium lasers that provided a devastating close range barrage.
yangsThoughts: Unlike the original mech, this one is designed for long range, and with that light engine it can mount enough weaponry to match an Atlas

details: The Sentinel is a medium BattleMech built by Defiance Industries as an infantry support and installation defense 'Mech, originally under exclusive contract for the Lyran Commonwealth. While initially pleased to have a then-Terran Hegemony controlled factory manufacturing the 'Mech exclusively for the LCAF, the Lyrans were less so when a Star League Council amendment in 2652 permitted the Star League Defense Force to purchase a highly advanced version of the design which Houses Steiner, Marik and Davion would not receive until years later. The Star League Sentinel carried a somewhat unique and highly advanced armament for its time. Its primary weapon was a Kawabata Weapons Industries UAC/5 developed especially for the 3L, and is capable of firing at double the rate of a standard AC/5 at the cost of slightly increased possibility of jamming the weapon until it can be overhauled. The Sentinel also mounted an advanced Defiance Streak SRM-2, the launcher designed to only fire if it gains a lock, saving ammo until the entire salvo is virtually guaranteed to hit. Unfortunately, the weapon's benefits were hampered by the companion small laser, the swap from the fragile Defiance A-1 for the sturdier Defiance B-1A moved the launcher's ammo feed by half a meter, resulting in ammo jams during abrupt maneuvers. Though MechWarriors discovered that they could clear a jam in the field by thumping the 'Mech's chest with its right fist, opponents quickly learned to concentrate fire on any Sentinel performing this, forcing pilots to retreat from battle anyway.
yangsThoughts: This thing was pretty good in its day, but UAC/5 are not special anymore so this thing could really use some upgrades

details: The Sentinel is a medium BattleMech built by Defiance Industries as an infantry support and installation defense 'Mech, originally under exclusive contract for the Lyran Commonwealth. While initially pleased to have a then-Terran Hegemony controlled factory manufacturing the 'Mech exclusively for the LCAF, the Lyrans were less so when a Star League Council amendment in 2652 permitted the Star League Defense Force to purchase a highly advanced version of the design which Houses Steiner, Marik and Davion would not receive until years later. The Royal version of the Sentinel featured a significantly upgraded weapon loadout, mounting a Gauss rifle in place of the autocannon and a medium laser in place of the Streak SRM-2 rack, but retaining the small laser. To handle the weight of the Gauss rifle it was almost entirely rebuilt using an XL engine and endo steel structure, with seven and a half tons of ferro-fibrous plate enhancing its protection.
yangsThoughts: This is one of the lightest mechs to get a Gauss rifle out onto the field, so if you want a nice long range punch, this is the mech to do it with.

details: TThe Starslayer was intended to replace the Crab as the Star League's standard medium 'Mech after Cosara Weaponries were unable to meet the SLDF's required production volumes. Though Blue Shot's design won selection in 2765, the Starslayer would also be plagued with technical delays with its tracking system that would delay full production until 2772 and the Amaris Civil War. Despite the SLDF's desperate need for matériel, tensions between General Kerensky and Archon Robert Steiner II would result in Blue Shot delaying SLDF shipments until 2781 due to lack of 'up-to-date' shipping information and as a result the Starslayer would be far more common in the Lyran Commonwealth Armed Forces service than the SLDF. By now a thoroughly Lyran Commonwealth design, production of the original 2C Starslayer would cease during the technological decline of the early Succession Wars. The discovery of the Helm Memory Core and the desperate rearmament race spawned by the attacking Clans would lead Blue Shot Weaponry to resume production with the superior and updated STY-3C model in wake of the initial Clan Invasion.[6][3] The resurrected Starslayer would go on to become a mainstay of both the Lyran Alliance and Lyran Commonwealth Armed Forces as a optimal raider, its mobility and largely energy based loadout allowing it to stay in the field nearly indefinitely. The application of Lyran and Clan Sea Fox built Clan-tech weaponry has only enhanced its abilities yet further and the Starslayer figured prominently in Task Force Kingbreaker during the Dark Age era. The Clan Invasion era Starslayer is built on a lightweight Thor-4 Endo Steel chassis and reaches a top speed of 86.4 km/h with its Magna 250 fusion engine. The Starslayer also has five Odin's Own Model 34z jump jets which give it the ability to jump up to one hundred and fifty meters. Finally, the design carries nine and a half tons of Norse-34 ferro-fibrous with CASE. The CASE protects the BattleMech from complete destruction in the event of an ammunition explosion. The primary weapons on the Starslayer are a pair of BlazeFire Systems Large Lasers that provide the 'Mech with a striking distance of up to four hundred and fifty meters. For close range combat, the Starslayer has two Defiance B3M medium lasers and a Defiance B4M small laser. Finally, to allow the Starslayer to find and exploit weak points in an enemy's armor, it also carries a TharHes 4 Pack SRM-4 launcher.
yangsThoughts: Similar to a Crab, as it was intended to be, this thing mounts a nice array of energy weapons, but I can't help thinking it could do more with some Clan-spec gear.

details: The Talon was first deployed in 2670 to provide light BattleMech companies much needed long-range firepower and punch. Star League High Command was so impressed by the Talon during its combat trials in 2669, they immediately ordered the 'Mech into full production. The BattleMech was so wildly successful that sales to the Star League Defense Force quickly surpassed all other light BattleMechs. The Talon is intended to act as a long-range sniper for light 'Mech units. To carry out this mission, the upgraded TLN-5W Talon was equipped with a Lord's Light 2 ER PPC that gives it a reach of just under 700 meters. For defense at close ranges, the Talon has two ChisComp 39 medium lasers that give it enough protection to defend itself from most other light 'Mechs. Talon BattleMechs are also fleet of foot, with a top speed of 129.6 km/h, allowing it to keep up with almost any light 'Mech in existence, and is able to reach this speed through the use of an Edasich Motors 280-rated XL engine. The 'Mech is armored with seven and a half tons of Kallon Light Shield A standard armor, which provides the highest amount of protection against enemy fire possible with standard, combat-grade armor. It also has 11 double heat sinks to handle the high heat load from its weapons payload.
yangsThoughts: Fast, good firepower, and good armor, an excellent example of a light 'Mech. If we've got some to spare, a Clan-spec PPC would really improve this design's performance.

yangsThoughts: The only difference between the 5V and 5W is the PPC, so the techs can easily give you whichever one you want with no trouble.

yangsThoughts: Even more free tonnage means you can get more firepower on here than you can with the original design, let me know if you want the techs to swap some things out for more firepower.

details: The Thunder Hawk was built by Norse-Storm BattleMechs in 2771, during the final six years of the SLDF's drive to retake the Terran Hegemony. The Thunder Hawk epitomizes the doctrine of firepower over maneuverability with its heavy weapons arsenal and low top speed. Taken out of production after the end of the Amaris Civil War, Thunder Hawk production began again during the mid-3050s.[5][6] Norse-Storm attempted, in the wake of the Clan Invasion, to make it their flagship design, though trouble manufacturing enough 'Mechs and parts to meet demand weakened its market share over decades afterwards. The Thunder Hawk rumbles through the battlefield at a maximum speed of 54 km/h. Despite the low speed, the Thunder Hawk is powered by a light weight Vlar 300 XL engine that weighs half as much as a standard fusion engine and allows the 'Mech to carry a heavier than normal weapons payload. The 'Mech is protected by nineteen and a half tons of armor, giving it more armor than the venerable Atlas. Additionally, because the Thunder Hawk has very little heat buildup from its weapons, it carries only ten heat sinks. The Thunder Hawk is armed to engage an enemy at stand-off ranges when facing any opposition. The weapons that enable the Thunder Hawk to achieve this are a trio of Norse-Storm Model 7D Gauss rifles fed by six tons of ammo split evenly across the torso locations. When combined, these main guns can cripple or outright destroy most lighter 'Mechs in only a few short salvos. To defend itself either when an enemy closes to short range or its Gauss ammo runs dry, the Thunder Hawk has an impressive array of four Defiance B3M medium lasers: Two in the left arm, one in the right, and one in the head.
yangsThoughts: Three full Gauss rifles on a single mech, this thing is a monster of long range firepower. I bet we could make it crazy if we used Clan-spec rifles, we might even be able to get 4 of them on there.

The Thunder Hawk epitomizes the doctrine of firepower over maneuverability with its heavy weapons arsenal and low top speed. The Thunder Hawk rumbles through the battlefield at a maximum speed of 54 km/h. The 'Mech is protected by nineteen and a half tons of armor, giving it more armor than the venerable Atlas. Additionally, because the Thunder Hawk has very little heat buildup from its weapons, it carries only ten heat sinks. A simple upgrade of the Thunder Hawk, the 7Y removes the four medium lasers and replaces them with two Medium Pulse Lasers. This increases the accuracy of the close range firepower while sacrificing some of the sheer brute force that the 'Mech can use at short ranges. This helps when facing much lighter and more maneuverable enemies.
yangsThoughts: A simple change that improves accuracy, I think they should have taken off some of the Gauss ammo to get a third pulse laser on though.

details: The Wyvern was designed in 2660 as an urban warfare BattleMech for the Star League Defense Force. While considered slow when compared to other medium 'Mechs, and indeed vulnerable if forced to engage in open country, the Wyvern quickly proved itself to be one of the best urban combatants ever built. Thanks to its combination of weaponry, heavy armor and jump jets the Wyvern can take on and destroy 'Mechs well outside its weight class. Standard practice during battle was for the Wyvern to use its impressive maneuverability to move around obstacles other 'Mechs couldn't navigate and get behind an enemy to unleash considerable firepower on their exposed rear. Unfortunately the Wyvern proved to be too good at its job, which included not just urban defense and garrison duty but crowd control as well; few MechWarriors relished the task of pacifying a city and threatening unarmed protestors. Despite the stigma associated with it there were some MechWarriors who enjoyed piloting the Wyvern; they were amongst the proudest in the SLDF. The Wyvern carries an eclectic, but effective, mix of weapons. The primary weapon used is a Nightwind Large Laser, an old but extremely reliable weapon, in its right arm. All of the laser's components are battle-tested and quite sturdy, but are so bulky that it makes the Nightwind one of the largest military lasers ever built. The Nightwind is backed up by a pair of Starflash Small Lasers in the same location. These were later additions, but in a testament to the large laser's robustness they are able to draw power directly from the Nightwind's primary feed without any loss in efficiency for any of the weapons. The lasers are backed up by a Jackson Dart-10 LRM-10 launcher, and a Totschlagen-6 SRM-6 launcher. The Wyvern achieves its great maneuverability through the use of four Northrup 750 jump jets, one of each mounted in either side torso and upper legs, giving it a total jumping distance of 120 meters. It is also well protected with nine and a half tons of Kilosh 1000 armor, made possible by the use of an Endo Steel frame. Its slow top speed of 65 km/h and cruising speed of 43 km/h means the Wyvern has trouble escaping from an opponent if caught in the open, although in a built-up cityscape how fast a 'Mech can run is less of an issue.
yangsThoughts: It's a good urban and mountainous terrain ambusher, but don't try to run it in open terrain, it just doesn't have the speed or armor for straight up brawls.

details: The Wyvern was designed in 2660 as an urban warfare BattleMech for the Star League Defense Force. While considered slow when compared to other medium 'Mechs, and indeed vulnerable if forced to engage in open country, the Wyvern quickly proved itself to be one of the best urban combatants ever built. Thanks to its combination of weaponry, heavy armor and jump jets the Wyvern can take on and destroy 'Mechs well outside its weight class. Standard practice during battle was for the Wyvern to use its impressive maneuverability to move around obstacles other 'Mechs couldn't navigate and get behind an enemy to unleash considerable firepower on their exposed rear. Unfortunately the Wyvern proved to be too good at its job, which included not just urban defense and garrison duty but crowd control as well; few MechWarriors relished the task of pacifying a city and threatening unarmed protestors. Despite the stigma associated with it there were some MechWarriors who enjoyed piloting the Wyvern; they were amongst the proudest in the SLDF. This upgrade, introduced concurrently with the original and utilized by the SLDF Royal brigades, uses an XL engine for power. That change enabled an upgrade of the large laser to a pulse laser version and one of the small lasers to a medium laser, while an additional heat sink helps keep down the extra heat created by these weapons. The Wyvern achieves its great maneuverability through the use of four Northrup 750 jump jets, one of each mounted in either side torso and upper legs, giving it a total jumping distance of 120 meters. It is also well protected with nine and a half tons of Kilosh 1000 armor, made possible by the use of an Endo Steel frame. Its slow top speed of 65 km/h and cruising speed of 43 km/h means the Wyvern has trouble escaping from an opponent if caught in the open, although in a built-up cityscape how fast a 'Mech can run is less of an issue.
yangsThoughts: A significant upgrade, that XL engine really frees up the mech to be upgraded quite a bit.

details: The Apollo is designed to be a long range fire support BattleMech. Armed with massive missile launchers which can rain destruction down on an enemy, the Apollo shouldn't have to worry about its less than average speed if it is employed correctly. The Apollo uses a pair of Zeus LRM-15 launchers with Artemis IV fire control systems to give the 'Mech increased missile accuracy. These two launchers with their advanced fire control system can, on average, do almost as much damage as a pair of LRM-20 launchers. These are backed up for close combat by a pair of Sunglow Prism-Optic small pulse lasers which almost feel as if they were added to the 'Mech as an afterthought. Pilots should ensure they remain near bodyguards or on the edge of the battle, as the mounted 220-rated engine gives it a top speed of only 64.8 km/h. 11.5 tons of standard armor give it some survivability should the Apollo take return fire or be engaged by closer foes.
yangsThoughts: A solid basic fire support mech, this thing isn't gonna be winning any speed records or destroying all the enemies by itself, but it's a dependable support.

details: The Apollo is designed to be a long range fire support BattleMech. Armed with massive missile launchers which can rain destruction down on an enemy, the Apollo shouldn't have to worry about its less than average speed if it is employed correctly. The Apollo uses a pair of Zeus LRM-15 launchers with Artemis IV fire control systems to give the 'Mech increased missile accuracy. These two launchers with their advanced fire control system can, on average, do almost as much damage as a pair of LRM-20 launchers. The 2S was built to address the concerns about short range defense that MechWarriors who pilot the Apollo had. This modification removes the two small pulse lasers from the 'Mech and replaces them with two medium lasers, giving the Apollo a more powerful close range punch. Pilots should ensure they remain near bodyguards or on the edge of the battle, as the mounted 220-rated engine gives it a top speed of only 64.8 km/h. 11.5 tons of standard armor give it some survivability should the Apollo take return fire or be engaged by closer foes.
yangsThoughts: A bit better if it gets attacked behind the lines, but you still don't want it be getting shot at if you can avoid it.

details: The Apollo is designed to be a long range fire support BattleMech. Armed with massive missile launchers which can rain destruction down on an enemy, the Apollo shouldn't have to worry about its less than average speed if it is employed correctly. The 3T variant of the Apollo is a major modification. The 'Mech downgrades its LRM-15 launchers to a pair of LRM-10 launchers with Artemis IV fire control systems, but upgrades its close range weaponry to two medium pulse lasers and two medium lasers.
yangsThoughts: Way better at close range, but it loses out on the fire support, I prefer to just not let my fire support units get shot at personally.

details: The Apollo-C is a complete ground up refit sold by Clan Sea Fox, after they managed to pick up a bunch of completely wrecked APL-1Ms for cheap. They rebuilt the mechs using XL engines and endo-steel chassis and then replaced the two LRM-15s with eight Clan-spec LRM-5s (4 in each torso). This weight savings combined with the other upgrades allowed installation of a Clan-spec medium pulse laser and ER medium laser behind hidden panels in each arm, while also allowing for 2 more tons of reloads to give mechwarriors plenty of alternate ammunition space. It even allowed for the engine to be upgraded to a 275 rating to improve the mech's speed to 86km/h. 'All these changes massively improved the Apollo-Cs ability to protect itself at short range, and made it extremely effective at luring raiders in with the apparent prospect of an undefended fire support mech, only to suprise them with a face full of LRMs that have no minimum range.
yangsThoughts: Now this, THIS is what I mean when I tell people that Clan-spec weaponry turns a good mech design into a great one. This thing is even more effective at close range damage output, and yet still maintains more long range firepower than the original design. They even managed to make it faster, reducing one of the biggest weaknesses of the original design.

details: The Hyperion was developed in response to the Omega's debut by the Word of Blake during the Jihad. Using Clan tech to make the structure and engine allowed for a massive 72.5 tons of available pod space for the Omnimech. Propelled by a 375 XL engine and built on an Endo-Steel framework the designers did have to cut some slight corners on the actual armor to allow for such extensive firepower, the mech is protected by 18.5 tons of ferro fibrous armor, which is one and a half tons below the maximum for the frame.  The PRIME configuration mounts twin Gauss Rifles, twin ER PPCs, twin Improved Heavy Large Lasers, twin ER Medium Lasers and a seemingly superfluous ER Small Laser in the head.
yangsThoughts: This thing is efficient, extremely heavily armed and surprisingly fast for how big it is, it's a marvel of engineering. You might want to consider replacing those small lasers with some more armor or heatsinks though, something this big will attract a lot of fire.

details: The Hyperion was developed in response to the Omega's debut by the Word of Blake during the Jihad. Using Clan tech to make the structure and engine allowed for a massive 72.5 tons of available pod space for the Omnimech. Propelled by a 375 XL engine and built on an Endo-Steel framework the designers did have to cut some slight corners on the actual armor to allow for such extensive firepower, the mech is protected by 18.5 tons of ferro fibrous armor, which is one and a half tons below the maximum for the frame.  The A configuration mounts twin Hyper Assault-Gauss 40s, and 4 ER Large Lasers giving it a solid punch in direct fire..
yangsThoughts: This thing is efficient, extremely heavily armed and surprisingly fast for how big it is, it's a marvel of engineering. Those Hyper Assault Gausses are bad news, but it might be useful to downgrade the lasers and put more weaponry on.

details: The Hyperion was developed in response to the Omega's debut by the Word of Blake during the Jihad. Using Clan tech to make the structure and engine allowed for a massive 72.5 tons of available pod space for the Omnimech. Propelled by a 375 XL engine and built on an Endo-Steel framework the designers did have to cut some slight corners on the actual armor to allow for such extensive firepower, the mech is protected by 18.5 tons of ferro fibrous armor, which is one and a half tons below the maximum for the frame.  The B configuration mounts four UAC/20s, with 1 ER large laser the only ranged weapon.
yangsThoughts: This thing is efficient, extremely heavily armed and surprisingly fast for how big it is, it's a marvel of engineering. The big problem with this design is the lack of ammo for the UAC's, only two tons of ammo each will mean you run out of ammo too fast. Maybe just get rid of the laser and put another 4 tons of ammo?

details: The Hyperion was developed in response to the Omega's debut by the Word of Blake during the Jihad. Using Clan tech to make the structure and engine allowed for a massive 72.5 tons of available pod space for the Omnimech. Propelled by a 375 XL engine and built on an Endo-Steel framework the designers did have to cut some slight corners on the actual armor to allow for such extensive firepower, the mech is protected by 18.5 tons of ferro fibrous armor, which is one and a half tons below the maximum for the frame.  The C configuration mounts two gauss rifles and 4 LRM 20's boosted by Artemis IV.
yangsThoughts: This thing is efficient, extremely heavily armed and surprisingly fast for how big it is, it's a marvel of engineering. A massive bombardment layout, this thing can sustain two full minutes of barrage.

details: The Hwatcha is the most recently developed super heavy mech for missile focused brawling. It has a fairly large engine and 18 tons of ferro fibrous armor to keep it safe regardless of battling distance. There are three main variants of the mech, designed for short medium and long range. The short range variant mounts a massive array of 12 Clan-spec Streak SRM-6 launchers backed by two Heavy PPCs in the torsos.
yangsThoughts: An interesting design, a massive array of short range missiles that can rip many smaller mechs to pieces, but you're gonna run out of ammo fast if you don't watch the bins.

details: The Hwatcha is the most recently developed super heavy mech for missile focused brawling. It has a fairly large engine and 18 tons of ferro fibrous armor to keep it safe regardless of battling distance. There are three main variants of the mech, designed for short medium and long range. The medium range variant mounts 4 MRM 40s in the arms and pairs them with twin light ppcs in the torsos for taking long range potshots while getting in range for the missiles.
yangsThoughts: As long as you can get into range and can supply it with ammo, this thing will rip anything it gets close to into pieces.

details: The Hwatcha is the most recently developed super heavy mech for missile focused brawling. It has a fairly large engine and 18 tons of ferro fibrous armor to keep it safe regardless of battling distance. There are three main variants of the mech, designed for short medium and long range. The long range variant mounts 6 clan-spec LRM-20s and 2 clan-spec lrm 10s backed by twin light PPCs. This variant has been criticised by its operators for not having sufficient CASE mounts to cover all the ammo bins.
yangsThoughts: An insane level of long range fire support, the clan spec launchers mean that it doesn't need to worry about things getting inside its range. Just make sure you don't blow up from the ammo.

details: The Snowflame is the latest Superheavy battlemech from Clan Ghost Bear. Equipped with two massive miniature mass drivers, this mech is able to inflict massive damage to anything unfortunate enough to get in range. This is at the cost of it being extremely slow to actually move.
yangsThoughts: Those mass drivers are seriously bad news, watch out for their poor aim though. You could always downgrade them and mount multiple smaller guns to improve overall firepower.

details: The Dillettante is a new super heavy chassis from the Federated Commonwealth, continuing their love of autocannons, it mounts four rotary autocannons, two class 5 and two class 2, backed up by some Clan-spec ER PPCs and Large Pulse Lasers. Since all of its weapons have fairly long range, the designers were able to support such heavy firepower by putting a very small 270 XL engine, making the mech behave somewhat more like a mobile turret than a unit that can actually give chase. Nevertheless, the sheer amount of firepower on the mech allows it to perform as an excellent anvil for more mobile lance mates to drive enemies onto.
yangsThoughts: This thing has a monstrous level of firepower, but I don't particularly like how much of the mech is occupied by volatile ammo.  I wouldn't go bringing this mech for any convoy escort or destruction missions either, as it's not gonna be able to catch them.

details: The Dillettante is a new super heavy chassis from the Federated Commonwealth, continuing their love of autocannons, it mounts two rotary autocannons, both class 5, backed up by some Clan-spec ER PPCs and Large Pulse Lasers. The P2 variant replaces the rotary autocannon 2s of the P1 variant with a clan sourced Hyper-Gauss Rifle for improved armor penetration. Since all of its weapons have fairly long range, the designers were able to support such heavy firepower by putting a very small 270 XL engine, making the mech behave somewhat more like a mobile turret than a unit that can actually give chase. Nevertheless, the sheer amount of firepower on the mech allows it to perform as an excellent anvil for more mobile lance mates to drive enemies onto.
yangsThoughts: This is like the old monkey's paw story, I wanted less explosive ammo and I got less explosive ammo, but now the GUNs can explode instead. I wouldn't go bringing this mech for any convoy escort or destruction missions either, as it's not gonna be able to catch them.

details: Although this simian-looking BattleMech was conceived as a Totem 'Mech for Clan Fire Mandrill, the Inner Sphere gave it the reporting name Baboon and saw it first in Jade Falcon second-line units. Since then it has proven to be much more widespread among Clan second-line forces and recon units. It is relatively fast for a 'Mech its size, topping out at 119 km/h. Its good speed and armament is made possible by the use of an endo steel structure and three tons of ferro-fibrous armor. The Howler is, essentially, a missile boat, very potent for its size. Meant to provide fire support to light garrison Stars, the 'Mech carries three LRM-5 launchers and three tons of ammunition. It is also used extensively as a training 'Mech.
yangsThoughts: A decent fire support mech for the weight, but very easily killed if it doesn't keep moving.

details: Although this simian-looking BattleMech was conceived as a Totem 'Mech for Clan Fire Mandrill, the Inner Sphere gave it the reporting name Baboon and saw it first in Jade Falcon second-line units. Since then it has proven to be much more widespread among Clan second-line forces and recon units. It is relatively fast for a 'Mech its size, topping out at 119 km/h. Its good speed and armament is made possible by the use of an endo steel structure and three tons of ferro-fibrous armor. The Howler was redesigned by Clan Steel Viper scientists to improve their light second-line units after the Clan was expelled from the Inner Sphere by Clan Jade Falcon. A smaller extralight engine provides the space for four ATM-3s along with two tons of ammunition. Though the 'Mech's running speed is reduced, it uses MASC to accelerate to 130 km/h in short bursts.
yangsThoughts: This thing has significantly more firepower than the first form, and enough free tonnage to run a good loadout, just keep in mind it's still fragile.

details: Although this simian-looking BattleMech was conceived as a Totem 'Mech for Clan Fire Mandrill, the Inner Sphere gave it the reporting name Baboon and saw it first in Jade Falcon second-line units. Since then it has proven to be much more widespread among Clan second-line forces and recon units. It is relatively fast for a 'Mech its size, topping out at 119 km/h. Its good speed and armament is made possible by the use of an endo steel structure and three tons of ferro-fibrous armor. The standard Howler chassis has had its LRM-5s removed and replaced with six ER medium lasers, fitted in triple mounts in each side torso.
yangsThoughts: Surprisingly good range and damage for direct fire here, but it overheats easily, so you're gonna need to disengage frequently to avoid issues with heat.

details: The Bowman started off production as a defensive artillery platform with a pair of Arrow IVs, however, general Clan dislike of indirect fire combat, and the lack of direct combat options on the original design resulted in several redesigns. This variant abandons any hope of the Bowman being an ArtilleryMech. Both artillery launchers are replaced with LRM-20s, the legs are equipped with anti infantry weaponry and 3 ER Medium Lasers, and an ER Large Laser give more direct fire options. More heat sinks are added to keep the heat in check.
yangsThoughts: A nice fire support mech with decent direct fire, and plentry of armor and ecm let it brawl well given that Clan LRMs have no minimum range anyway.

details: The Bowman started off production as a defensive artillery platform with a pair of Arrow IVs, however, general Clan dislike of indirect fire combat, and the lack of direct combat options on the original design resulted in several redesigns. Another of the direct fire designed variants, this one mounts a HAG 20 in the right arm, and and ATM 9 in the left torso to replace the LRM-20. Four ER Medium Lasers give it decent support for when the bins run dry.
yangsThoughts: Mostly a brawler at this point, this mech only mounts one indirect fire weapon.

details: The Conjurer, designated the Hellhound by the IS, is a medium to short range fighter. The mech is, for all intents and purposes, a Wolverine IIC however the Clans would not be willing to name a mech after Clan Wolverine, and so it was renamed. As a second line mech, most variants use a standard engine, but do mount an endo-steel chassis for reduced weight. This variant, however, also uses an extra-light engine to save as much weight as possible for weaponry. Mounting a quartet of heavy medium lasers, a medium pulse laser and twin Stream SRM-6s, this mech pushes the 14 double heatsinks well past their endurance. For anti-infantry work it also mounts two light machine guns.
yangsThoughts: Jump capable, heavily armed and surprisingly well armored for its size, this is one of the mechs that really showcases just how good clan weaponry really is.

details: The Conjurer, designated the Hellhound by the IS, is a medium to short range fighter. The mech is, for all intents and purposes, a Wolverine IIC however the Clans would not be willing to name a mech after Clan Wolverine, and so it was renamed. As a second line mech, most variants use a standard engine, but do mount an endo-steel chassis for reduced weight. This variant, however, also uses an extra-light engine to save as much weight as possible for weaponry. A very late variant produced for the Wolf Empire, this version mounts a mix of clan-spec and Inner Sphere weaponry. It downgrades the engine to a 250-xl and uses the weight savings to mount a rotary AC/5, MASC, Clan Stream SRM-6 and two Clan medium pulse lasers.
yangsThoughts: This variant is interesting, the big heavy RAC/5 is unusual to find on a mech this size, but gives it far more firepower than an AC20 without the short range.

details: The Grizzly is a BattleMech that was developed by Clan Ghost Bear scientists in the thirtieth century. Unfortunately, the design excels at no one thing, so it never carved a niche in the Clan's touman. Thirteen tons of armor are mounted on an Endo Steel frame. Jump jets are mounted so that the Grizzly can leap up to 120 meters at a time. Aside from the 'jack of all trades, master of none moniker,' the Grizzly is also poorly engineered. Slagged armor tends to get stuck in joints and weapons, while the actuators are difficult to repair in their own right. Production ceased in 3013 and the remaining 'Mechs were relegated to green Provisional Garrison Clusters.  The Grizzly actually packs a solid long-range punch with a Omega 12-coil Gauss rifle and a Type X Short Bow LRM-10 Launcher. At closer ranges, a brace of pulse lasers provides accurate fire in the form of a Kolibri Delta Series Large Pulse Laser, Kolibri Delta Series Medium Pulse Laser, and a Chi Series Small Pulse Laser.
yangsThoughts: If it weren't for the production quality issues, this mech would have been a solid design with good firepower at all ranges. However, the lack of focus in the firepower holds it back a bit.
